title: baphiastrum pilosum
intro: Baphiastrum pilosum, commonly known as the hairy Baphiastrum or hairy Baphia, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The therapeutic actions of Baphiastrum pilosum include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and alleviate respiratory issues, likely due to its bioactive constituents, which include alkaloids and flavonoids. However, I couldn't verify the exact list of bioactive constituents, so further research is needed for a comprehensive understanding of its properties.

constituents: Baphiastrum pilosum has many active constituents, such as alkaloids like pilosin, pilosine, and pilosamine, as well as flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids like protocatechuic acid and ferulic acid. These compounds have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to improve daily life by reducing the risk of infections, alleviating pain and swelling, and protecting against cell damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the flavonoids and phenolic acids in Baphiastrum pilosum have been shown to have vasodilatory and cardio-protective effects, which can help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease. parts: Baphiastrum pilosum has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal properties. The roots of Baphiastrum pilosum are rich in alkaloids, such as pilosine and pilosinine, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The flowers of Baphiastrum pilosum are a source of terpenoids, including pilosol and pilosene, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
side_effects: Baphiastrum pilosum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. In extreme cases, consuming the plant's leaves or roots can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers due to its toxic properties.
